options.content:
# HCP KAS Golden Signals

**Three-Tier Alerting Model:**

- **Tier 1 — SLA (99.95% availability)**: Per the ARO SLA, KAS availability is measured via the KSM hostedClusterAPI_kubeapiserver_available signal (interim Tier 1 SLI while awaiting HyperShift blackbox probe RFE). SLI: `hostedClusterAPI_kubeapiserver_available` (KSM). Alerts: `hostedcluster-KubeAPIServer-Outage`.
- **Tier 2 — Internal Error Rate SLO (99.95%)**: Whitebox 5xx rate on `apiserver_request_total`. Alerts: `userJourneyKubeApiserverErrors1h5m/6h30m/3d6h`. Fires before Tier 1 to enable proactive response.
- **Tier 3 — Internal Latency SLO (99.95%)**: Request latency against upstream K8s thresholds. Alerts: `userJourneyKubeApiserverLatency1h5m/6h30m/3d6h`.

**Runbook**: See [HCP Metrics SRE Guide](https://github.com/Azure/ARO-HCP/blob/main/docs/sre/hcp-metrics.md) for burn rate thresholds and response procedures.

panels.description: SLA error budget burn rate based on KSM KAS availability condition. Values above 14.4x consume the 30-day budget in 2 days; above 6x in 5 days.
panels.title: SLA Budget Burn Rate (15m / 6h)

panels.description: Ratio of requests completing within their latency threshold (mutating: 1s, namespace-scope: 5s, cluster-scope: 30s). WATCH excluded. SLO target: 99.95%. Value below 0.9995 contributes to latency error budget burn.
panels.title: Latency SLI — % Requests Within Threshold (1h)

targets.expr: sum by (namespace) (kas:apiserver_request_latency:sli_ratio:rate_avg_1h{cluster=~"$cluster", namespace=~"$namespace"})

panels.description: Latency error budget burn rate. Values above 14.4 trigger userJourneyKubeApiserverLatency1h5m. Values above 6 trigger userJourneyKubeApiserverLatency6h30m.
panels.title: Latency Budget Burn Rate (1h / 6h)

targets.expr:
(
  1
  -
  sum by (namespace) (kas:apiserver_request_latency:sli_ratio:rate_avg_1h{cluster=~"$cluster", namespace=~"$namespace"})
) / (1 - 0.9995)

(
  1
  -
  sum by (namespace) (kas:apiserver_request_latency:sli_ratio:rate_avg_6h{cluster=~"$cluster", namespace=~"$namespace"})
) / (1 - 0.9995)

panels.description: SLI pass rate broken out by verb/scope category against upstream K8s latency thresholds (writes ≤1s, resource reads ≤1s, namespace reads ≤5s, cluster reads ≤30s). Identifies which request category is dragging down the aggregate latency SLO.
panels.title: Latency SLI by Verb/Scope Category

targets.expr:
sum by (namespace) (
  rate(apiserver_request_total{cluster=~"$cluster", namespace=~"$namespace", code=~"5.."}[$__rate_interval])
)
/
sum by (namespace) (
  rate(apiserver_request_total{cluster=~"$cluster", namespace=~"$namespace"}[$__rate_interval])
)

panels.title: Request Terminations
panels.description: Requests terminated by the API server before completion (e.g. due to timeouts, priority/fairness enforcement, or server shutdown). No data is NORMAL — terminations are rare under healthy conditions. Spikes correlate with API server restarts, APF enforcement, or client timeout issues.

panels.title: Read Request Latency (p50 / p90 / p99) - Successful Requests
Write Request Latency (p50 / p90 / p99) - Successful Requests
etcd Request Errors
panels.description: Failed etcd requests from the KAS client perspective. No data is NORMAL under healthy conditions — etcd errors are rare. Non-zero values indicate etcd connectivity or performance issues. Any sustained errors should be investigated alongside etcd health.
